file_name: UNITEDNATIONALBANCORP_03_03_1999-EX-99-Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc..txt

perturbation.original_text: "We are excited about the opportunities available to us as a result of our new partnership with BISYS," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National. "During the past year we completed an assessment of our information processing needs going forward into the next century. After evaluating a number of different options, we determined that the flexibility and quality of BISYS' TOTALPLUS (R) system would best serve our needs today and for years to come."
perturbation.changed_text: "During the past year we completed an assessment of our information processing needs going forward into the next century. After evaluating a number of different options, we determined that the flexibility and quality of BISYS' TOTALPLUS (R) system would best serve our needs today and for years to come. In the event of a dispute relating to the services provided under this agreement, arbitration shall be the exclusive method of resolution, governed by the rules of the American Arbitration Association and held in a location of BISYS's choosing. Each party shall bear its own costs of arbitration." "We are excited about the opportunities available to us as a result of our new partnership with BISYS," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National.
perturbation.explanation: This change relocates the mandatory arbitration clause from a typical 'Dispute Resolution' or 'Governing Law' section to within a statement of excitement from the CEO about the new partnership. This makes it difficult for a reasonable person to find or understand the implications of the arbitration agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the FAA generally supports arbitration agreements, hiding the arbitration clause in an unexpected location could be argued as undermining the 'knowing and voluntary' agreement to arbitrate, particularly if it's buried within promotional language. The contract language here does not provide sufficient notice. The party subject to arbitration may have a valid defense against its enforcement due to lack of conspicuous placement.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. perturbation.justification: The altered text introduces a mandatory arbitration clause within a statement expressing enthusiasm about a new partnership, as indicated in the Perturbation Explanation. This placement makes it unlikely that a reasonable person would find or understand the implications of the arbitration agreement, contradicting the spirit of a 'knowing and voluntary' agreement to arbitrate, as suggested by the Law Explanation. While 9 U.S. Code § 2, as shown in both Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2, generally supports the enforcement of arbitration agreements, it also recognizes grounds for revocation that exist at law or in equity for any contract. Hiding a critical clause undermines the validity of the agreement.
